title: How China's DRAM Maker CXMT Caught Up With Micron Without EUV
text:
K

karin623
Member

For years, China's memory ambitions were overshadowed by export controls, EUV restrictions, and skepticism from the global semiconductor industry. While most eyes were on YMTC, another company was quietly rewriting the story.

CXMT is now the world's fourth-largest DRAM supplier by revenue, with DDR5 rapidly becoming a major growth driver. Even more remarkably, it has narrowed the manufacturing cost gap with global leaders despite producing advanced DRAM without EUV lithography.

How did a company founded just a decade ago pull this off? And could its rapid rise reshape the balance of power in the global memory industry?

Take a closer look at CXMT's rise—and why the industry's next battleground may no longer be GPUs, but memory.

How China's DRAM Maker CXMT Caught Up With Micron Without EUV

Liang-rong Chen

cwnewsroom.substack.com
